constructs: affective intensity attention allocation quantity-sensitivity preference-construction
claim: Value functions reshape from linear to step-like when preference construction switches from quantitative calculation to affective cue-reading.

constructs: executive-capacity cognitive-load self-regulation temporary-impairment inhibition-updating dissociation
claim: Executive control draws on a limited pool of cognitive resources that depletes transiently; subsequent control attempts fail unless the resource replenishes, and different control types share selectively.

phenomenon: enhanced emotional intensity during anticipation versus retrospection
claim: Anticipation of future events produces more intense current emotion than retrospection about past events due to more extensive mental simulation, independent of objective uncertainty.
Subjective difficulty during information processing serves as a metacognitive alarm that triggers shift from intuitive to deliberate reasoning.

Repetition effects decompose into a frequency-insensitive lexical component and a frequency-attenuated episodic component; frequency attenuation depends entirely on episodic involvement, not lexical dynamics.

Spacing effects in cued-memory tasks arise from differential rehearsal and vanish under incidental learning; spacing effects in free recall persist across learning conditions via automatic temporal-context encoding.
